rulings.Case Title: PETER TURNER VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: AMENDED MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Motion For Summary Judgment Filed By City And County Of San
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Thursday, September 11, 2014, Line 3, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's AMENDED MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Granted. The plain language of CCP sec. 526a explicitly provides that a tax must be assessed against an individual in order for him or her to have taxpayer standing. Defendants met their initial burden by providing evidence showing that a San Francisco tax was never assessed against Plaintiff. (Defendants' Undisputed Material Facts 1-9.) Plaintiff has not raised a triable issue of material fact as to whether a San Francisco tax was assessed against him. Plaintiff cites no cases that establish that payment of a "pass through" tax conveys taxpayer standing. (See Cornelius v. Los Angeles County (1996) 49 Cal.App.4th 1761, 1777-78, rejecting the plaintiff's contention that payment of sales tax confers taxpayer standing because "[s]ales taxes are generally construed to be taxes on the retailer, not the consumer to whom the retailer passes the burden;" Torres v. City of Yorba Linda (1993) 13 Cal.App.4th 1035, 1048, holding that an increase in sales tax paid by customers because of an increase in tax on the retailers does not establish taxpayer standing because "the tax was imposed on the person who sold the goods.") In any event, even if payment of a "pass through" tax did convey standing, Plaintiff submits no admissible evidence establishing that he ever paid a "pass through" tax. In his declaration, Plaintiff states that he "believe[s]" that his rent was increased because his landlord was passing through an increase in property tax. (Declaration of Peter Turner, par. 8.) "Evidence that gives rise to no more than mere speculation cannot be regarded as substantial, and is insufficient to establish a triable issue of material fact." (Sangster v. Paetkau (1998) 68 Cal.App.4th 151, 163.) Further, the Equal Protection argument as to CCP sec. 526a was recently considered and rejected in Wheatherford v. City of San Rafael (2014) 226 Cal.App.4th 460. The court held that the statute is subject to the rational basis test, requiring a plaintiff to show that it serves "no conceivable state purpose." (Id. at 469.) "Courts have noted that it is not irrational to limit standing in taxpayer lawsuits¿We also see a rational purpose in limiting taxpayer standing to persons who pay property tax in the jurisdiction corresponding to the public entity defendant. Individuals who have directly paid a tax to the government have obtained a sufficiently personal interest in the illegal expenditure of funds by county officials to become dedicated adversaries." (Id.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: CAHILL CONTRACTORS, INC. VS. A&B PAINTING, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Thursday, September 11, 2014, Line 12, INTERVENOR ST. PAUL FIRE & MARINE INSURANCE COMPANY's MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION The motion is denied without prejudice. The Court determines that the trial judge should rule on the duty to defend issue and how the defense costs should be apportioned. Denying the motion will avoid the practical difficulties of sorting out multiple, and potentially conflicting, duties to assume the active defense of litigation in progress.See Crawford v. Weather Shield Mfg. Inc. (2008) 44 Cal.4th 541, 565, fn.12. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: JI YANG et al VS. HEBREW HOME FOR THE AGED, DISABLED DBA JEWISH HOME et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Tax Costs
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Thursday, September 11, 2014, Line 5, PLAINTIFFS JI YANG, ALELI SAN JUAN, IRMANETTE DE ROSAS, MILENN MARIANO and MARLYN DATAR's Motion To Tax Costs Motion to Tax Costs is granted in part and denied in part. The Motion is denied as to the costs to expedite Plaintiff's deposition because, in the absence of any contrary evidence produced by Plaintiffs, Defendant's evidence is sufficient to establish that this was a necessary cost. The Motion is granted as to the process fees of $388. Plaintiff raised an issue as to the propriety of these charges and Defendant failed to address or even argue in opposition that the charges were accurate and proper. Motion is moot as to the $90 charge for transcripts as the revised Memorandum of Costs excludes the item. Motion is denied as to the charges for messenger services. Counsel's declaration stating that the charges were necessary for numerous reasons is sufficient evidence to find the charges necessary. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: JI YANG et al VS. HEBREW HOME FOR THE AGED, DISABLED DBA JEWISH HOME et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Attorney'S Fees And Costs
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Thursday, September 11, 2014, Line 5, DEFENDANT HEBREW HOME FOR THE AGED, DISABLED DBA JEWISH HOME's Motion For Attorney'S Fees And Costs Motion for Attorneys' Fees and Costs is denied. Given the lack of evidence presented in connection with the instant Motion, the Court is unable to determine whether Plaintiffs' claims were "frivolous, unreasonable or groundless." (Rosenman v. Christensen, Miller, Fink, Jacobs, Glaser, Weil & Shapiro (2001) 91 Cal.App.4th 859, 868; see also Jersey v. John Muir Med. Ctr. (2002) 97 Cal.App.4th 814, 831 [defendant not entitled to award of fees and costs merely because summary judgment was granted in its favor].) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. rulings.Case Title: ZIAD SLEIMAN VS. DJAMOL GAFUROV et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Compel Further Responses To Disco
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Thursday, September 11, 2014, Line 13, PLAINTIFF CLAIRE FAHRBACH's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Discovery Granted in part and denied in part. As to the Special Interrogatories: #41 is granted. Defendant to provide code complaint responses. Within one week of this order, Defendant shall provide all requested user reviews without any personal information. Within one week of receipt, Plaintiff shall send a list of users for which Plaintiff is requesting name and contact information. Within one week of receipt of the list, Defendant shall provide name and contact information for identified users on list. #102 is denied. Defendant responded to this interrogatory. #103 is denied. The interrogatory is overbroad. As to the Document Requests: #30 is granted. Defendant to provide code-complaint responses/responsive documents that show a breakdown of ratings for customer service, efficiency, professionalism, and safety for the driver as of the date of the incident. #33 is denied. Defendant has offered to provide GPS data from its App reflecting the trip request in progress of the time of the subject accident if plaintiff signs a protective order. If plaintiff wants this information, she must sign a protective order. #39, 49, 50, and 51 are denied as overbroad. Sanctions are denied. The Court finds that the meet and confer efforts were sufficient. The parties are required to meet and confer regarding a protective order before the hearing. The parties shall bring an agreed-to form of a protective order to the hearing. Amended responses and respnsive documents to be provided within 15 days of Notice of Entry of this Order. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: JENNIFER TAN VS. NOUR EL HOUDA TANVERAKUL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT / Notice
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Thursday, September 11, 2014, Line 6, DEFENDANTS NRT WEST, INC. and NRT LLC's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT The court denies the motion for summary judgment. There is a triable issue of material fact regarding whether Ms. Tanverakul was acting within the course and scope of her employment with defendant when the alleged tortious conduct occurred. At 9:23-11:22 and 31:12-32:21, Mr. Chung explains that he contacted Ms. Tanverakul because he believed that she may have made some sort of error with regard to the purchase of the 325 26th Ave property. (See also Lieu depo, 54:9-16). That contact was directly related to defendant's business and a trier of fact could find that the subsequent meeting where the alleged defamation and other torts occurred was a natural outgrowth of the broker/agent business relationship. There is a triable issue of material fact regarding whether the common interest privilege of CC 47(c) applies. The court liberally construes plaintiff's evidence on summary judgment. The trier of fact can infer Ms. Tanverakul's malice (ill will/reckless disregard of the truth) from the following: (1) Ms. Tanverakul could have harbored ill will because she was accused of bungling the real estate transaction based plaintiff's flyer; (2) Ms. Tanverakul screamed at plaintiff and threatened to report her to the real estate board; and (3) Ms. Tanverakul had a financial incentive to drive a wedge between plaintiff and Mrs. Lieu. (Tan depo., Opposition to UMF 31). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: SAMBIT BEHERA VS. ARK.COM, A DELAWARE CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Compelling Further Response To Interrogatories And Imposing Monetary Sanction
rulings.Rulings: Matter ON CALENDAR FOR Thursday, September 11, 2014, Line 8, DEFENDANT ARK.COM, INC. Motion For Order Compelling Further Response To Interrogatories And Imposing Monetary Sanction. Pro Tem Judge Dennis Hyde, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: SANDRA LEON VS. KRISTIN KOJAN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Judgment On The Pleadings
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Thursday, September 11, 2014, Line 8, DEFENDANT KRISTIN KOJAN's Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings Granted with 20 days leave to amend. Plaintiff is given leave to amend to allege facts supporting her contention that Defendant recklessly instructed her to perform an exercise that went beyond the range of ordinary activities involved in providing personal training services to a person of plaintiff's age, weight, conditioning, and experience. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: JHONA MATHEWS VS. THE ARCHDIOCESE OF THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order Re Defendant Monsignor James Tarantino'S Deposition
rulings.Rulings: Matter ON CALENDAR FOR Thursday, September 11, 2014, Line 7, PLAINTIFF JHONA MATHEWS Motion For Protective Order Re Defendant Monsignor James Tarantino's Deposition. Pro Tem Judge Dennis Hyde, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. Defendants must bring to the hearing a statement from the deponent's treating physician as to the specific factors creating the necessity of having the deposition on September 12, 2014 and otherwise as specifically outlined in email to the parties on September 9, 2014. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. =(302/JPT)
